content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

August 10, 1979


Mr. Michael Storper
Sacramento Representative
Friends of the Earth
717 K Street, #208
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Storper:

I am responding to your letter of July 23, 1979 concerning the procedures surrounding the adoption of conflict of interest codes. During the past week, I have tried unsuccessfully to reach you by telephone on several occasions. If you will call me at your convenience, I would be happy to explain the process for adopting and amending conflict of interest codes.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim, Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

TP:nrw

FRIENDS OF THE EARTH
CALIFORNIA LEGISLATIVE OFFICE
717 K STREET, #208, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 446-3109

July 23, 1979


Mr. Michael Bennett, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 - K Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Dear Mr. Bennett:

     I has come to our attention that each subdivision of
the state is required to submit its own guidelines for
declaration of property holdings of employees or directors
in order to determine potential conflicts of interest.

We would like to know:

a.  What criteria and evaluation procedures does FPPC
    employ in deciding whether a particular agency
    conflict-of-interest guideline should be
    approved?

b.  Is there a procedure by which the FPPC can
    reverse a decision to approve such guidelines?

c.  Is there a procedure by which the public can
    request reversal of prior certification of such
    guidelines.

We would appreciate having this information as soon as
possible.

     Thanking you in advance for your consideration, I am

Sincerely yours,

Michael Storper
SACRAMENTO REPRESENTATIVE

sections.question:
a. What criteria and evaluation procedures does FPPC employ in deciding whether a particular agency conflict-of-interest guideline should be approved?

b. Is there a procedure by which the FPPC can reverse a decision to approve such guidelines?

c. Is there a procedure by which the public can request reversal of prior certification of such guidelines.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks what criteria and procedures the FPPC uses to approve agency conflict-of-interest guidelines, whether the FPPC can reverse prior approvals, and whether the public has a mechanism to request reversal of previously certified guidelines.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or answer section present. Document contains clear question section with three sub-parts (a, b, c). Poor OCR quality in header section but body text is legible.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from Sacramento Representative Michael Storper to FPPC Executive Director Michael Bennett seeking information about the FPPC's procedures for approving, reversing, and allowing public challenge of agency conflict-of-interest guidelines.
